Package recoder.util

Interface HashCode

All Superinterfaces:
Equality
All Known Implementing Classes:
AbstractIndex, IdentityIndex, NamedModelElement.LexicalOrder, NaturalIndex, Order.CustomLexicalOrder, Order.Identity, Order.Lexical, Order.Natural, ProgramElement.TreeStructure, Relations.ModelElementLexicalOrder
public interface HashCode extends Equality
This interface defines a function mapping objects to integer values. The integer code is used for hashing algorithms and must obey the constraint
equals(x, y) implies hashCode(x) == hashCode(y).
Therefore, this interface extends that equality relation. The value returned by hashCode does not need to be a unique value, but should be different for most different objects to achieve adequate performance. Whether or not null objects are allowed is up to the specific implementation.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final HashCode
    IDENTITY
    Identity hash code implementation.
    static final HashCode
    NATURAL
    Natural hash code implementation.

    Description
    int
    hashCode(Object x)
    Return an integer value representing the object.

    Methods inherited from interface recoder.util.Equality

    equals

  • Field Details

    • NATURAL

      static final HashCode NATURAL
      Natural hash code implementation. This object calls and returns x.hashCode() and therefore does not allow null objects.

    • IDENTITY

      static final HashCode IDENTITY
      Identity hash code implementation. This object calls System.identityHashCode(x) which will report zero for null objects.

  • Method Details

    • hashCode

      int hashCode(Object x)
      Return an integer value representing the object.
      Parameters:
      x - an object.
      Returns:
      the hash code.
